title: Operation Ghost Proxy
brief: Draft and deliver a spear-phishing lure targeting an Orion Logistics employee. Harvest their corporate email password.

debrief.success: Password harvested. That credential opens the staging segment for the next contract. Stay off-grid until the lure page expires.
debrief.fail: You burned the session. Orion's SOC will flag the domain and warn their users. If you retry, pick your lure more carefully.

emails.subject: Lure sent — Orion training portal (read once)
emails.body:
Operator—

Spear-phishing lure is delivered to the target mailbox. They should see a plausible "session refresh" page on a typo-domain we control.

What is phishing? In short: fake messages or sites that impersonate a trusted party so the victim types a password into the attacker's form. We are not breaking cryptography here — we are catching what they submit.

When the harvest hits, use that material as staging trust on your local SSH surface before the gateway hop will authenticate you.

— Sable

emails.subject: Operator brief — Ghost Proxy (slot confirmed)
emails.body:
Operator—

You are cleared for staging segment access only.

Rules of engagement:
- No destructive payloads on production billing paths.
- If Orion SOC escalates, acknowledge spoof/laylow before retrying noisy steps.
- Submit evidence through the encrypted handler channel only.

If this message reaches the wrong mailbox, burn the session and report.

— Sable, Handler

emails.subject: Automated retention notice
emails.body: This channel is monitored. By continuing you accept cross-border arbitration under Helix Standard Terms R-12. Logs may be retained seven years for disputes. Nothing in this notice waives operator indemnities.

emails.subject: Staging topology (last verified — unofficial)
emails.body:
Unofficial routing: local → gw-edge → app-api → db-core.

Edge requires staged trust before connect. Do not rely on this map for warranty claims; graph is authoritative in-game.

objective.summary: Harvest the target's corporate email password via spear-phishing lure.

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: Staging SSH client — establish operator trust material before the edge gateway accepts your session (not a second compromise of local)

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: CVE-2024-6387 (OpenSSH regreSSHion) — unauthenticated RCE class on affected versions

files.content: Failed key attempts from app-api. Maintenance account still enabled.
Reminder: rotate the app maintenance artifact after the quarter close.

artifact.description: A maintenance credential artifact that can unlock alternate access paths to app-api in this game.

services.name: http
services.vulnRef: SSTI / OGNL-style template injection (cf. CVE-2022-26134 class — server-side template RCE)

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: SSH auth surface — alternate path when maintenance key artifact is available (no CVE; access gating)

files.content: stacktrace: query endpoint retries against db-core failed at 00:16

INTERNAL — ticket search API builds SQL with string concat (title = '" + user + "'). Red-team note: try sql demo / sql translate in the wargame to see how your input maps to psql. Fix = prepared statements + least privilege DB role.

services.name: postgres
services.vulnRef: CVE-2019-9193 (PostgreSQL COPY FROM PROGRAM) — dangerous COPY / superuser misconfig class

files.content: Do not expose manual adjustments before quarter close.
